title: Coniogramme javanica
taxonomy: Coniogramme javanica is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Coniogramme, which is a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. Coniogramme javanica is a type of creeping fern, known for its small, delicate fronds and ability to thrive in shaded areas. The plant is native to Southeast Asia, including countries such as Indonesia and the Philippines. Coniogramme javanica is often grown as an ornamental plant in tropical gardens due to its attractive and low-maintenance nature.
uses: Coniogramme javanica is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits. intro: Coniogramme javanica, also known as Java fern or Java maidenhair, is a type of tropical, terrestrial fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coniogramme javanica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coniogramme javanica is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in capsule or powder form, making it a popular ingredient in dietary supplements and herbal remedies.
